content:
To find the minimum value of $b$ across all "pupusa" pairs, we need to consider the conditions given.

Since we require $a < 391$ and $\text{lcm}(a,b) > \text{lcm}(a,391)$, the first step is to understand the relationship between $\text{lcm}(a,b)$ and $\text{lcm}(a,391)$.

Since $391 = 17 \times 23$, if we can find a number $b$ that includes a factor not present in either $a$ or 391, then $\text{lcm}(a,b) > \text{lcm}(a,391)$.

However, for a pair to be a "pupusa" and $391 \mid \text{lcm}(a,b)$, if $a = 391k$ where $k$ is an positive integer, we must choose $b$ so that the $\text{lcm}(a,b) > \text{lcm}(a, 391)$. Since $\text{lcm}(a, 391) = 391$, then we need to find the smallest integer $b$ such that $391b \mid (391*k)$ but $(391*k, b) \nmid 391$

If $a = 391k$, the first possible "pupusa" pair occurs when $k = 1$, so $a = 391$ and $\text{lcm}(a, b)$ is greater than $\text{lcm}(391, b)$.

When $a = 391$, then $\text{lcm}(391, b)$ will be $b$, as there are no common prime factors between $391(17 \times 23)$ and $b$. Therefore $\text{lcm}(391, b)$ is greater than $\text{lcm}(391, b)$ only when $b = \text{lcm} (391, b)$

To determine the minimum "b" then we can determine that $391 \mid \text{lcm}(391,b)$ so b needs to be the smallest number such that the $\text{lcm}(391,b) > 391$ (i.e. $\text{lcm}(391,b) = \text{lcm}(391 \times m, b)$). However since $\text{lcm}(391 \times m, b)$ is greater than $391b$ since there are shared prime factors, this is possible in that the shared factor is "b".

In any event we want the minimum number for "b" such that  for $391 \mid \text{lcm}(391, b)$  $\text{lcm}(391, b) = b\text{ or lcm}(391, b) > b$.  We know that $b > a$; and the smallest value $b$ that can be is $\text{lcm}(a,b)$ for a pupusa. Thus, if $\text{lcm}(a,b) > \text{lcm}(a,391)$ then it follows $\text{lcm}(391, b) > b$.
